title: VALUES clause with 2 integers leads to Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031
description:
Hello everyone,
as mentioned in the title the usage of VALUES clause with 2 integers and using these integers to compare values in a FILTER clause (Query below) leads to an internal error:
Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031: SPARQL compiler: Internal error: ssg_print_rvr_fixed_val(): no SPART_VARR_FIXED
What I have tried which doesn't lead to an error but is semantically equal:

Using a fixed integer instead of a variable for either ?x and/or ?y
Using BIND instead of VALUES for ?x and/or ?y also works, e.g. BIND(100 AS ?x) before FILTER

Here is the query:
PREFIX  bsbm: <http://www4.wiwiss.fu-berlin.de/bizer/bsbm/v01/vocabulary/>
PREFIX  rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX  rdf:  <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX  bsbm-inst: <http://www4.wiwiss.fu-berlin.de/bizer/bsbm/v01/instances/>

SELECT  ?product ?label
WHERE
  { VALUES ( ?productType ?productFeature1 ?productFeature2 ?x ?y ) {
      ( bsbm-inst:ProductType1 bsbm-inst:ProductFeature142 bsbm-inst:ProductFeature144 100 400 )
    } #variables
    ?product  rdfs:label            ?label ;
              rdf:type              ?productType ;
              bsbm:productFeature   ?productFeature1 ;
              bsbm:productPropertyNumeric1  ?p1
    FILTER ( ?p1 > ?x )
    ?product  bsbm:productPropertyNumeric3  ?p3
    FILTER ( ?p3 < ?y )
    OPTIONAL
      { ?product  bsbm:productFeature  ?productFeature2 ;
                  rdfs:label           ?testVar
      }
    FILTER ( ! bound(?testVar) )
  }
ORDER BY ?label
LIMIT   10

Virtuoso version 07.20.3229 on Darwin (x86_64-apple-darwin14.5.0), Single Server Edition on MacOS Mojave 10.14.4
Has anyone encountered this issue yet?
Best regards
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE products (id INT PRIMARY KEY, label VARCHAR(255), productType VARCHAR(255), productFeature1 VARCHAR(255), productPropertyNumeric1 INT, productPropertyNumeric3 INT);
INSERT INTO products (id, label, productType, productFeature1, productPropertyNumeric1, productPropertyNumeric3) VALUES (1, 'Product A', 'ProductType1', 'ProductFeature142', 150, 350);
INSERT INTO products (id, label, productType, productFeature1, productPropertyNumeric1, productPropertyNumeric3) VALUES (2, 'Product B', 'ProductType1', 'ProductFeature144', 200, 450);
SELECT label FROM products WHERE productPropertyNumeric1 > 100 AND productPropertyNumeric3 < 400;
SELECT label FROM products WHERE productPropertyNumeric1 > 150 AND productPropertyNumeric3 < 400 LIMIT 10;
key_bug_features: VALUES clause with two integers in FILTER comparison
